GitHub Actions workflow for building, generating provenance, creating releases, and publishing to PyPI.

# Purpose
This GitHub Actions workflow file automates the process of building, verifying, and publishing a software package when a new tag is pushed to the repository. The workflow consists of several jobs: `build`, `provenance`, `create-release`, and `publish-pypi`. The `build` job runs on the latest Ubuntu environment, checks out the code, sets up the necessary tools, builds the project, and generates a hash of the build artifacts. The `provenance` job depends on the `build` job and uses the SLSA framework to generate provenance information, ensuring the integrity and authenticity of the build. The `create-release` job creates a draft release on GitHub using the artifacts from the `build` job. Finally, the `publish-pypi` job publishes the package to PyPI, using the artifacts and metadata generated in the previous steps.
